zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley於1413年出生在英國波克夏斯特拉特費爾德 莫特時，他的父親Sir Roger De Mortimer25歲，母親Elizabeth24歲。他和Jane Hebert至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1460年12月30日在英國越可爾桑達爾 馬尼亞享年47歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾馬特利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley was born in 1413, in Stratfield Mortimer, Berkshire, England, his father, Sir Roger De Mortimer, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 24. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Jane Hebert. He died on 30 December 1460, in Sandal Magna, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in Martley, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley est né en 1413, à Stratfield Mortimer, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger De Mortimer, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Jane Hebert. Il est décédé le 30 décembre 1460, à Sandal Magna, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Martley, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley 1413 in Stratfield Mortimer, Berkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Roger De Mortimer 25 und seine Mutter Elizabeth 24. Er hatte mit Jane Hebert mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 30. Dezember 1460 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Sandal Magna, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Martley, Worcestershire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martleyが1413年にイギリスバークシュアイアストラトフィールド モーティマーで生まれた時，父Sir Roger De Mortimerは25歳，母Elizabethは24歳であった。彼はJane Hebertとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1460年12月30日にイギリスヨークシュアイアサンダル マグナで47歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアマルトリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley(이)가 1413년 영국 버크실르 스트라트필드 모르팀어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger De Mortimer(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Jane Hebert(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년 12월 30일 영국 요르크실르 샌다 매그나에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 마틀리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley nasceu em 1413, em Stratfield Mortimer, Berkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger De Mortimer, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Jane Hebert. Ele faleceu em 30 de dezembro de 1460, em Sandal Magna, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Martley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley родился в 1413 г. в местности стратфиелд мортимир, биркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Roger De Mortimer было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 24. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Jane Hebert. Он умер 30 декабря 1460 г. в местности сэндаль магна, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности мартли, ворсестиршир, Англия.
